seoTitle: How to Set Up Logic Jumps in Typeform
metaDescription: Learn how to configure Logic Jumps in Typeform to create conditional branching and guide respondents to relevant questions.
intro: Logic Jumps let a Typeform direct respondents to the most relevant next question instead of forcing everyone through the same sequence. This guide covers how to set up Logic Jumps, build conditional branching rules, and combine them with hidden fields and variables for more personalized forms.

steps.heading: Open the Logic Builder
steps.body: Access the Logic section of your Typeform editor to start creating jump rules.

steps.heading: Create Logic Jump rules
steps.body: Set conditions that determine which question a respondent sees next based on how they answered a previous one.

steps.heading: Test conditional branching
steps.body: Walk through the form with different answers to confirm the Logic Jumps direct respondents to the correct sections.

steps.heading: Publish and verify the logic flow
steps.body: Publish the form once testing confirms the jumps work as intended, and periodically verify the flow still makes sense as the form evolves.

faq.question: What is a Logic Jump in Typeform?
faq.answer: A Logic Jump is a rule that sends a respondent to a specific question based on how they answered a previous one, creating conditional branching.

faq.question: Can Logic Jumps skip entire sections of a form?
faq.answer: Yes, Logic Jumps can be set up to skip past irrelevant questions entirely based on a respondent's answers.

faq.question: Can I combine Logic Jumps with variables and hidden fields?
faq.answer: Yes, combining these features allows for more advanced personalization within the form.

faq.question: Do I need to test Logic Jumps before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, testing multiple answer paths helps confirm the jumps route respondents correctly before the form goes live.
